query: Baikal Ice Marathon on Skis cross country ski race Lake Baikal

videos.title: Extreme race competition on frozen Lake Baikal
videos.description:
(19 Feb 2017) The deepest - and possibly oldest - freshwater lake in the world hosted a freezing endurance race on Sunday. 
Baikal in Siberian Russia is the largest (by volume) freshwater lake in the world and in the winter temperatures regularly drop below -14 Centigrade (7 Fahrenheit). 
More than 40 Russians and 30 Dutch nationals took part in the event.
They competed in an ice skating and cycling contest, with the skiing contest sadly cancelled due to a lack of people willing to brave the gruelling journey on skis. 
The racing lasted three days and its participants had to overcome a total of 205 kilometres (127 miles). 
Strong winds, extreme cold and unpredictable ice conditions were the main challenges for the athletes. 
On the second day of racing a heavy blizzard forced several of the weaker competitors to quit the event. 
The winners from the two categories were - unsurprisingly - Russian. 
Ivan Timofeyev from Irkutsk took the win in the skating race and in the cycling Alexander Ulitinov, who is also from Irkutsk, claimed the honours. 
Both completed the race in just over 10 hours.

videos.title: Simon Carey and Sark Kenny Lake Baikal Ski Trek 2013
videos.description:
In mid February 2013 two British adventurers will travel to Siberian Russia and ski trek 400 miles across the frozen surface of the worlds oldest and deepest lake.​
​​
​One of the goals of this expedition is to raise enough money to install and maintain a public water tank as a source of clean water in Kibera slum, Nairobi, Kenya. This will be made possible through the generosity of our sponsor Hitachi Capital.

Read more...http://www.adventuresinbetween.com/

videos.title: 'Ice Storm' endurance race held in Siberia
videos.description:
(19 Feb 2017) 'ICE STORM' ENDURANCE RACE TAKES PLACE IN SIBERIA
The deepest - and possibly oldest - freshwater lake in the world hosted a freezing endurance race on Sunday. 
Baikal in Siberian Russia is the largest (by volume) freshwater lake in the world and in the winter temperatures regularly drop below -14 Centigrade (7 Fahrenheit). 
More than 40 Russians and 30 Dutch nationals took part in the event.
They competed in an ice skating and cycling contest, with the skiing contest sadly cancelled due to a lack of people willing to brave the gruelling journey on skis. 
The racing lasted three days and its participants had to overcome a total of 205 kilometres (127 miles).
